snippets.title: Inertia and silhouette for choosing k
snippets.code:
from sklearn.cluster import KMeans
from sklearn.metrics import silhouette_score
from sklearn.preprocessing import StandardScaler
import numpy as np

X = StandardScaler().fit_transform(np.random.randn(300, 4))

for k in range(2, 9):
    km = KMeans(n_clusters=k, n_init=10, random_state=0)
    labels = km.fit_predict(X)
    print(f"k={k} inertia={km.inertia_:.2f} silhouette={silhouette_score(X, labels):.3f}")
snippets.explanation: Plot inertia for the elbow and compare silhouette peaks across k. Always scale features before Euclidean clustering metrics.

snippets.title: Per-sample and per-cluster silhouette
snippets.code:
from sklearn.metrics import silhouette_samples, silhouette_score
from sklearn.cluster import KMeans
import numpy as np

labels = KMeans(n_clusters=3, random_state=0).fit_predict(X)
print("mean:", silhouette_score(X, labels))
for c in np.unique(labels):
    mask = labels == c
    print(f"cluster {c}:", silhouette_samples(X, labels)[mask].mean())
snippets.explanation: `silhouette_samples` reveals weak clusters dragging down the global mean.

snippets.title: External metrics with ground-truth labels
snippets.code:
from sklearn.metrics import (
    adjusted_rand_score,
    normalized_mutual_info_score,
    homogeneity_completeness_v_measure,
)

y_true = [0, 0, 1, 1, 2, 2]
y_pred = [1, 1, 0, 0, 2, 2]  # cluster IDs permuted

print("ARI:", adjusted_rand_score(y_true, y_pred))
print("NMI:", normalized_mutual_info_score(y_true, y_pred))
h, c, v = homogeneity_completeness_v_measure(y_true, y_pred)
print(f"homogeneity={h:.3f} completeness={c:.3f} v={v:.3f}")
snippets.explanation: ARI and NMI are invariant to cluster ID renaming. Use only when reference labels exist for evaluation.
